title: oxalis bojeriana
intro: Oxalis bojeriana, also known as the Madagascar wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, which can be attributed to its high concentration of oxalic acid. The health benefits of Oxalis bojeriana include its potential use as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
